name: Chicken and Sweet Pepper Stir-Fry
recipeInstructions:
1. Thinly slice the chicken into 1/2 inch pieces. Place in a bowl; stir in soy sauce and sherry. Let stand for 30 minutes.
2. Spray a cold wok or large skillet with Pam; preheat over medium-high heat.
3. Add onion, stir-fry for 2 minutes.
4. Add peppers, stir-fry for 1 minute.
5. Add mushrooms, stir-fry for about 1 more minute, or until veggies are crisp-tender. Remove veggies from wok and set aside.
6. Drain chicken, reserving the marinade. Add oil to the wok. Add gingerroot; stir-fry for 15 seconds.
7. Add half the chicken, stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until no longer pink. Remove.
8. Stir-fry remaining chicken for 3-4 minutes until no longer pink. Return all chicken, veggies, and bamboo shoots to the wok.
9. Push everything from the center of the wok.
10. Stir broth, cornstarch, and 1/4 tsp pepper into the reserved marinade; add to the wok. Cook and stir until slightly thickened, tossing gently to coat the chicken mixture. Serve while hot.
